title: valeriana sambucifolia var. fauriei
intro: Valeriana sambucifolia var. fauriei, also known as a variant of the Korean valerian or Kwan-mul, is a plant species within the Valeriana genus native to East Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include a sedative effect, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it a potential treatment for anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. The health benefits of Valeriana sambucifolia var. fauriei include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to support sleep and promote overall well-being.
